_doc: LOCAL-ONLY backlog for the in-place Ralph loop (gitignored; the shell owns task status — the agent must NOT edit this file). Schema: docs/HARNESS.md §8. All tasks are verifiable with NO live paid-API calls.

tasks.title: Validation gates: declare contracts on real jobs so gates fire
tasks.do: T006 built the gate MECHANISM but no real job declares contracts, so zero gates fire. Declare produces/consumes ArtifactContracts on the perfumes + places pipeline stages. Each contract is SHAPE + NON-EMPTY: assert the produced artifact exists, is non-empty, and has its expected fields/columns (e.g. the Takeout CSV has its expected headers; a parsed perfume has name + notes; a resolved place has a place_id) — enough to catch real format drift without brittle full-schema checks. Verify against cached data/ fixtures — NO live calls.
tasks.doneWhen: Perfumes + places stages declare produces/consumes shape+non-empty contracts; gates derive and fire between stages (pipeline start-log gate count > 0); a unit test proves a satisfied contract passes and a drifted/empty one fails the consumer; no live API/scrape in verification.

tasks.title: Validation gates: surface on the DAG graph + failure logs
tasks.do: Represent validation gates on the pipeline DAG graph: render a gate marker on the producer->consumer edge in a pipeline RUN, make a FAILED gate visually distinct (e.g. red), and make the gate-failure run/logs reachable by clicking it. Expose gate state via the API so the dashboard can render it. Gates show only on a pipeline RUN, never on the structure-only /pipelines/[name] view.
tasks.doneWhen: Gates appear on the pipeline-run DAG between producer->consumer; a failed gate is visually distinct and links to its failure logs; gates do NOT show on the structure-only /pipelines/[name] graph; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Per-item progress: emit ctx.progress(i/N) + per-item logs across all jobs
tasks.do: Jobs that process N items don't report progress as they go — e.g. perfumes-find-url works 41 items but never logs `1/41 …` and the run % doesn't advance. Make every item-processing job emit `ctx.progress(i/N*100)` and a per-item log line as it works (sensible denominators), not just a final summary. Apply across ALL such jobs, not just find-url.
tasks.doneWhen: Item-processing jobs emit per-item progress + a per-item log line; the run % advances during the run (verified against cached fixtures / scratch DB, no live calls); covers every item-loop job.

tasks.title: Move the Chrome profile to a shared framework location
tasks.do: The persistent Chrome profile lives inside the perfumes job (src/jobs/perfumes/data/chrome-profile). Move it to a framework-level location — a top-level data/chrome-profile/ resolved in src/core/browser.ts and env-overridable (e.g. LOCALJOBS_CHROME_PROFILE) — so it isn't perfumes-specific and other scrapers (e.g. the places resolver) can reuse the warmed, trusted profile. Keep the new location gitignored; perfumes fetch keeps working against it.
tasks.doneWhen: The Chrome profile path is framework-level (top-level data/chrome-profile, env-overridable) not under src/jobs/perfumes/data; the new path is gitignored; perfumes fetch still uses it; typecheck + tests green.

tasks.title: Trim the README — it's a wall of text
tasks.do: The README is a wall of text. Trim it: REMOVE the ## Layout file-tree entirely, and present the worked-example pipelines compactly/scannably (a short line each, not paragraphs). Keep the essential what-it-is / how-to-run / ports / config content and the dashboard pages list. Aim for a noticeably shorter, scannable doc.
tasks.doneWhen: The Layout file-tree is gone; example pipelines are compact; the core what-it-is / how-to-run / install content remains accurate; the README is materially shorter.

tasks.title: Don't count cancelled runs as failed
tasks.do: Cancelled runs are currently lumped into the overview Failed tile. A cancelled run is a deliberate stop, not a failure. Stop counting cancelled as failed, and give it its OWN clickable Cancelled overview stat tile (filtering like the other tiles), consistent with how status is aggregated elsewhere.
tasks.doneWhen: Cancelled runs no longer count as Failed; a dedicated clickable Cancelled tile shows + filters them; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Dismissed vs ignored: park ignored items, show only on overview
tasks.do: Manually-ignored items (the Ignore action) currently reappear as stuck and inflate stuck counts on the pipelines tab + pipeline/job detail. There is ONE ignore concept (not two): stuck = automatic after repeated failures; ignored = a manual park. Fix the root cause so an ignored item STAYS ignored (ignore persists; the stuck query excludes ignored; a re-run does not resurrect it), is NEVER counted as stuck, and is shown ONLY on the overview via a new fifth Ignored tile (which filters to them).
tasks.doneWhen: Ignored items never count as stuck and don't appear outside the overview; a 5th clickable Ignored overview tile shows + filters them; an ignored item stays ignored across re-runs; unit tests cover persistence + exclusion-from-stuck.

tasks.title: Services: explicit 'remove cap' control
tasks.do: On the Services edit-limits form it's unclear how to clear a cap back to 'no cap' (inputs just show placeholder text). Add an explicit control (e.g. a 'No cap' / clear button per field) that sets the value to no-cap (null) unambiguously, instead of relying on typing/blanking.
tasks.doneWhen: Each limit field has a clear UI way to set it back to no-cap (null); saving persists null; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Services: colour free services green
tasks.do: On the Services page, colour the 'free' service pill green (paid stays as-is) so free vs paid reads at a glance.
tasks.doneWhen: Free-service pills render green; paid unchanged; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Keep cron schedule expressions on one line
tasks.do: Cron schedule expressions (e.g. perfumes `0 2 * * *`) wrap onto two lines even on a wide desktop. Keep the schedule on one line where possible (e.g. white-space:nowrap on the schedule cell/mono span). Keep this in mind for the mobile styling pass too.
tasks.doneWhen: Schedule cron expressions render on a single line on desktop; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Drop standalone jobs — everything is a pipeline
tasks.do: Remove the concept of standalone (non-pipeline) jobs: EVERY job must belong to a pipeline declared in an explicit *.pipeline.ts manifest (a single job = a one-stage pipeline WITH its own manifest — no implicit wrapping). Drop the dashboard Standalone jobs section, the job-level standalone schedule/enable path (pipelines own scheduling), and the registry/scheduler handling for non-member jobs. A job with no manifest is a config error — fail loud at load. Now that demo is gone there are no standalone jobs to migrate.
tasks.doneWhen: No standalone-job code path remains; every job must be declared in a *.pipeline.ts manifest (single-job pipelines included); a job with no manifest fails loudly at load; the dashboard has no Standalone section; scheduler/registry handle only pipeline-driven jobs; typecheck + tests green; docs updated.

tasks.title: Rename 'pipeline' → 'workflow' everywhere
tasks.do: Rename the pipeline concept to workflow everywhere: types (PipelineDefinition->WorkflowDefinition), DB tables (pipelines, pipeline_jobs, pipeline_runs, pipeline_run_logs -> workflow*) with a migration that PRESERVES existing rows, store functions, the discovery convention *.pipeline.ts -> *.workflow.ts (rename the existing local perfumes/places manifest files too, or they won't be discovered), pipeline-executor.ts -> workflow-executor, API routes (/api/pipelines, /pipeline-runs -> /api/workflows, /workflow-runs), dashboard routes/pages + all labels, the pipeline field on jobs, and all docs. Clean break — no old-name redirects needed. Mechanical but wide. Do this AFTER T037 so the model lands clean.
tasks.doneWhen: No 'pipeline' naming remains for the concept (code, DB tables, routes, UI, docs, *.workflow.ts files); a DB migration moves existing pipeline_* data to workflow_* without loss; typecheck + tests + dashboard build green; docs updated.

tasks.title: Programmatic input ingestion into a pipeline's data/raw
tasks.do: Add a way to push fresh input into a pipeline's data/raw without manual file copying. The mechanism (API endpoint vs CLI vs pull-from-source) is deferred to a human decision before building.
tasks.doneWhen: Mechanism decided with the human, then built so fresh input can be pushed into a pipeline's data/raw without manual copying.

tasks.title: Mobile dashboard styling pass
tasks.do: Make the dashboard responsive on mobile. Write an automated check that loads each dashboard page in a headless browser at an iPhone-17-class viewport (~402px wide) against a scratch DB and asserts basic mobile styling: no horizontal overflow, no text crossing element boundaries, no weird truncation/clipping. The in-place agent RUNS this check locally and records the per-page result in its worklog — it does NOT need to be added to CI. Then fix the responsive CSS (tables, stat cards, nav, DAG graph, schedule cells) until it passes.
tasks.doneWhen: An automated iPhone-17-viewport (~402px) check covers every dashboard page and asserts no horizontal overflow / no boundary-crossing / no truncation; the agent ran it locally and recorded the page-by-page outcome in .harness/worklog/T040.md; the dashboard passes it; dashboard build green.

tasks.title: Decide + wire up where pipeline output goes
tasks.do: Decide where pipeline output ultimately lands (Obsidian vault / static site / cloud sync) and build the step that delivers it there, instead of leaving output in data/out. The destination is a human decision first.
tasks.doneWhen: Destination decided with the human; the delivery step is built and writes output there.

tasks.title: More intuitive remote dashboard access (alias)
tasks.do: Make remote dashboard access friendlier than typing the tailnet IP:port on mobile — a Tailscale MagicDNS hostname, a `tailscale serve` nice URL, or a phone home-screen shortcut. Partly operational (Tailscale admin / the machine), not CI-verifiable.
tasks.doneWhen: The dashboard is reachable from the phone via a friendly alias/shortcut (human confirms).

tasks.title: Joint review of the perfumes pipeline
tasks.do: Walk through how the (overnight-built) perfumes pipeline actually works, together — it hasn't been reviewed jointly. A conversation, not a build.
tasks.doneWhen: The perfumes pipeline has been walked through together and any follow-ups captured.

tasks.title: Folder-hierarchy check-in
tasks.do: Review the repo's file/folder organisation together and confirm it's laid out the way the owner wants. Subjective, no pass/fail.
tasks.doneWhen: Repo layout reviewed together and confirmed / adjustments captured.

tasks.title: Dashboard styling — make it more joyful
tasks.do: Check in on the dashboard's overall look & feel and make it more joyful. Subjective design work needing the owner's eye.
tasks.doneWhen: Look & feel reviewed with the owner and agreed changes applied.

tasks.title: Investigate why the Claude CLI service has a per-minute rate limit
tasks.do: Investigate why the claude-cli service is configured with a per-minute rate limit (rate_per_minute). Work out whether that cap is actually necessary/correct — is the Claude CLI genuinely rate-limited per minute, or is the value arbitrary/leftover? Then decide whether to keep, adjust, or remove it.
tasks.doneWhen: The reason for the claude-cli per-minute rate limit is understood and a decision is recorded (keep / adjust / remove).

tasks.title: Backlog view: green done icon + in-progress indicator
tasks.do: On the dashboard Backlog page (/backlog), make the done status badge/icon GREEN (it currently renders neutral), and add a distinct IN-PROGRESS icon. Note: TASKS.json only has pending/done (no in_progress status), so derive in-progress client-side as the single next-eligible task — the first not-done task in array order whose dependsOn are all done and which is not gated (i.e. the one the loop builds next) — and badge it distinctly from plain pending. (If a richer live currently-building signal is wired later, prefer that.)
tasks.doneWhen: Done tasks show a green done badge/icon on /backlog; the next-eligible task shows a distinct in-progress icon; plain pending tasks stay neutral; the dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Overview: hide the Stuck-items section when empty
tasks.do: On the overview page, hide the entire "Stuck items" SECTION (its heading + list/empty-state) when there are zero stuck items — render the section only when there is at least one stuck item. Keep the Stuck stat TILE/count at the top regardless. Apply the same empty-section hiding to the Ignored section if it has its own dedicated section.
tasks.doneWhen: When nothing is stuck, the overview shows no "Stuck items" heading or section (just the count tile up top); when something is stuck the section renders as before; the dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Sweep the repo for lingering "pipeline" usage (rename to workflow)
tasks.do: After the pipeline→workflow rename (T038), do a COMPLETE repo scan for any remaining use of the word "pipeline" and convert it to "workflow" across the board — code identifiers, types, API routes, UI labels/headings, comments, and docs (README.md / CLAUDE.md / docs) / schema. EXCLUDE legitimate residual references: the DB migration that renames the old `pipelines`/`pipeline_*` tables MUST still name them to perform the rename — leave those; and node_modules, .git, data/, and historical .harness/worklog/ notes are out of scope.
tasks.doneWhen: A `grep -ri pipeline` over tracked source + docs (excluding the migration’s necessary old-table references, node_modules, data/, and worklog) returns nothing; every stray identifier/label/route now reads "workflow"; typecheck + tests + dashboard build pass.
